title: Tecoma chrysantha
taxonomy: Tecoma chrysantha is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Bignoniaceae. This family is a large group of flowering plants commonly known as the trumpet vine or catalpa family. The genus Tecoma is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Americas and are often referred to as the yellow bells. Tecoma chrysantha is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Tecoma chrysantha reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Tecoma chrysantha, also known as Yellow Bells, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. intro: Tecoma chrysantha, also known as Yellow Bells, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its clusters of bright yellow, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
